prevalence.source: ECCO and European IBD registry syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with improved survival and diagnosis

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic symptoms and flares

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus colonoscopy with biopsy; exclude infectious colitis

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with access to biologics and specialist IBD care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Colonoscopy and pathology widely available; standardized IBD pathways
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: 5-ASA, corticosteroids, immunomodulators, biologics and small molecules; colectomy for refractory disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long-term monitoring and adherence to therapy
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong IBD centers and access to advanced therapies
